research_question: Can pressure-increasing corner rows be decomposed without relabeling them as negative loss?
action_tried: Decomposed gross static pressure rise into hydrostatic, kinetic, straight/developing, pressure-recovery candidate, and available residual terms.
what_worked: 3/3 pressure-corner rows preserve finite gross static, hydrostatic, kinetic, and signed residual terms.
what_did_not_work: The rows did not become component-K, cluster-K, or F6-admissible coefficients.
analysis_why: Hydrostatic head explains the gross static rise; after hydrostatic and kinetic correction the available residual is small and negative, while reverse flow, component isolation, straight-reference, and same-QOI UQ gates fail.
scientific_use_now: Publication-safe diagnostic: section-effective pressure residual and pressure-recovery diagnostic.
next_action: Compare future evidence against the frozen pressure-corner result, not against a recomputed or clipped K.
admission_guardrail: No negative loss, no clipped K, no component-K, no F6 fit.

research_question: Can the current lower-right two-tap rows supply ordinary component-K evidence?
action_tried: Audited pressure/velocity basis, face-level q_ref, recirculation metrics, and component isolation for the current Salt2/Salt3/Salt4 endpoint pair.
what_worked: Endpoint basis and recirculation diagnostics are explicit and source-cited.
what_did_not_work: q_ref is rejected as a component-K denominator and component isolation remains apparent-cluster-only.
analysis_why: Aggregate reverse area fraction is about 0.763 and reverse mass fraction is about 0.500, so the endpoint pair is materially recirculating rather than ordinary single-stream flow.
scientific_use_now: Use as recirculating section-effective evidence; do not normalize into ordinary K.
next_action: Find same-topology low-recirculation anchor before reopening ordinary component-K review.
admission_guardrail: Component-K review requires RAF < 0.01 and RMF < 0.01 on a future anchor.

research_question: Can same-QOI UQ support coefficient admission from existing artifacts?
action_tried: Built a cross-family same-QOI admission table covering pressure delta, section/component K, F6 endpoints, PM10 recirculation, HTC/Nu, and heat-loss rows.
what_did_not_work: 0 rows were admitted; 0 component-K, 0 F6-fit, and 0 clipped-K rows.
analysis_why: Rows lack neighboring windows, accepted same-QOI mesh/GCI, raw sampler output, or thermal sign/policy support.
scientific_use_now: Use as canonical blocker table and evidence queue.
next_action: Use uq_gap_queue.csv to choose low-recirculation anchor harvest and F6 raw endpoint sampler rows.
admission_guardrail: Rows lacking neighboring windows or GCI remain blocked or diagnostic.

research_question: Can F6 endpoint rows advance from geometry to raw same-QOI pressure evidence?
action_tried: Reviewed six clean F6 endpoint pairs and twenty endpoint-face matrix rows.
what_worked: Clean ordinary-branch endpoint labels and face-matrix source paths are available.
what_did_not_work: Raw endpoint face sampler outputs, neighboring windows, and same-QOI mesh/GCI are still missing.
analysis_why: The existing F6 packages intentionally stopped before sampler output and before fitting; this prevents hidden F6 admission from geometry alone.
scientific_use_now: Use as implementation-ready sampler input, not F6 fit evidence.
next_action: Claim a raw endpoint face sampler row with exact retained/neighborhood windows and source paths.
admission_guardrail: No F6 fit until raw samples, time UQ, mesh/GCI, and ordinary-flow gates pass.

research_question: Can thermal HTC/Nu/heat-loss rows be merged into the same-QOI uncertainty logic?
action_tried: Carried thermal mesh-gate and row-ledger statuses into the same-QOI table.
what_worked: Thermal rows are visible alongside pressure/F6 rows with final-use and blocker labels preserved.
what_did_not_work: No internal-Nu, HTC, or heat-loss closure was admitted from this synthesis.
analysis_why: Current thermal evidence has sign-review, policy, diagnostic replay, or final-scorecard blockers and is not the fastest route to the pressure-corner publication claim.
scientific_use_now: Use as parallel gate status, not as pressure-corner admission evidence.
next_action: Defer to heat-loss phase rows unless pressure-corner progress needs thermal coupling context.
admission_guardrail: No runtime leakage from realized wallHeatFlux or imposed cooler duty.
